response.justification: Strong software engineering background with 20 years of experience across multiple domains and proven entrepreneurial ability, but fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirement of recognized AI expertise and strategic leadership. Recent AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq shows technical engagement but lacks the deep AI product development, research credentials, or strategic advisory experience required for a Director-level AI Strategy role.
strengths: 20 years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded systems, and infrastructure exceeds the 15-year requirement
Proven entrepreneur (GISCollective founder, 2016-present) and public knowledge sharer (FOSS4G 2019 speaker, workshop organizer) demonstrating platform-building and thought leadership capability
Recent engagement with AI ecosystem through AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, showing current awareness of agentic workflows
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML expertise or recognized AI products—recent work involves infrastructure tooling around AI agents, not core AI strategy, model development, or AI-powered product creation
Missing critical preferred qualifications: no Master's or PhD in AI/ML, limiting credibility to advise on cutting-edge AI adoption and innovation strategy

Career trajectory is execution-focused (Senior/Staff engineer roles) rather than strategic leadership; currently in career gap with no recent evidence of Director-level impact, strategic partnerships, or customer advisory work
